cross_service_analysis.note: Note: All methods used are elbv2 service methods
cross_service_analysis.explanation: All python_methods (describe_load_balancers, describe_listeners, describe_target_groups, describe_load_balancer_attributes) belong to elbv2 service. No cross-service issues found.
cross_service_analysis.recommendation: No action needed - rules correctly use elbv2 API methods

issues_fixed: Fixed emit structures to use items_for for array responses (describe_listeners, describe_load_balancers, describe_target_groups)
Fixed field paths to match emit structure (removed redundant Listeners[], LoadBalancers[], TargetGroups[] prefixes)
Added missing 'all' logical operators to conditions
Fixed discovery method for deletion_protection_enabled (changed from describe_listener_attributes to describe_load_balancer_attributes)
Fixed discovery dependencies - made describe_listeners and describe_load_balancer_attributes dependent on describe_load_balancers
Fixed TLS policy value to use proper SSL policy names in array format

test_results.notes: Validation complete. All rules pass metadata mapping validation. AWS scan executes successfully (0 checks found in test accounts - expected behavior as no load balancers exist).
